source.manual: MoWT Road Design Manual Vol 3 Part IV

description: Pavement rehabilitation design parameters from MoWT Vol 3 Part IV. Includes condition assessment criteria, deflection thresholds, overlay design procedures, and reconstruction triggers.

cracking.description: Percentage of road surface area exhibiting cracking
cracking.measurement: Visual survey per Appendix A methodology

rutting.description: Maximum rut depth measured with straight edge

patching.notes: High patching percentage indicates underlying structural problems

ravelling.notes: Indicates surfacing distress, not necessarily structural failure

deflection_criteria.measurement_method: Benkelman beam (standard in Uganda) or FWD

overlay_candidate.description: Deflection range suitable for overlay design
overlay_candidate.notes: Mean deflection. If max deflection > 2.0 mm, reconstruction may be needed.

reconstruction_trigger.description: Deflection indicating structural failure requiring reconstruction
reconstruction_trigger.notes: Combined with severe visual distress indicates reconstruction needed

temperature_correction.description: Deflection measurements must be corrected for pavement temperature
temperature_correction.notes: Uganda pavement temperatures typically 30-50 C. Correction per Appendix D.

deflection_procedure.description: Design overlay thickness from deflection data
steps: 1. Measure deflections along the road (Benkelman beam or FWD)
2. Correct for temperature to reference temperature (35 C)
3. Calculate representative rebound deflection (RRD) = mean + 2*std dev
4. Determine required SN_new for design traffic from Appendix E
5. Calculate effective SN_eff from deflection using Appendix F
7. Overlay thickness = SN_required / a1 (where a1 = 0.35 for AC overlay)

effective_analysis_procedure.description: Design overlay without deflection data, using visual condition + DCP
steps: 1. Assess existing pavement condition (visual survey + DCP)
2. Determine layer thicknesses from test pits or as-built records
3. Assign condition factors to each layer based on observed distress
4. Calculate effective SN from existing layers with condition factors
5. Determine required SN_new for design traffic

condition_factors.description: Reduction factors applied to layer coefficients based on condition
condition_factors.notes: Engineering judgment required. DCP penetration rate helps assess layer condition.

reconstruction_triggers.description: Conditions indicating full reconstruction rather than overlay

triggers.action: Severe structural distress -- overlay may not be adequate
Subgrade or base layer failure -- investigate before overlay

triggers.threshold: Subgrade CBR < design CBR after moisture damage

reconstruction_triggers.notes: Multiple triggers occurring together strongly indicate reconstruction. A single trigger may still permit overlay if other conditions are acceptable.

where.SN_new: Required structural number for new design traffic (inches, from Appendix E)
where.SN_eff: Effective structural number of existing pavement (inches, from Appendix F or deflection)
where.a1: Layer coefficient for overlay material (0.35 for AC per Table 5.2)

overlay_formula.notes: Minimum practical overlay thickness is 40 mm (AC14) or 50 mm (AC20)
